rulings.Case Title: RICARDO RIDEOUT VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, AN UNKNOWN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant City And County Of San Francisco'S Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, OCTOBER 23, 2007, LINE 11, THIS IS A LATE TENTATIVE RULING, Defendant City And County Of San Francisco's Motion For Summary Judgment IS GRANTED. 1)PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION BRIEF IS DEFECTIVE AS IT LACKS A STATEMENT OF UNDISPUTED FACTS. CCP SECTION 473c(b); 2) THE INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS CLAIM IS DENIED BECAUSE PLAINTIFF DID NOT COMPLY WITH THE PRE-FILING REQUIREMENT (GOVERNMENT CODE SECTION 911.2) AND DID NOT FILE A WRITTEN APPLICATION FOR LEAVE TO PRESENT A LATE CLAIM (GOVERNMENT CODE SECTION 911.4); 3)ASSUMING A PRIMA FACIE CASE OF AGE DISCRIMINATION, DEFENDANT HAS PROFFERED AMPLE EVIDENCE OF THE HIRING PROCESS AND THE REASONS FOR NOT RE-HIRING PLAINTIFF. PLAINTIFF DID NOT PROVIDE SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE OF DISCRIMINATORY ANIMUS OR PRE-TEXT; 4)PLAINTIFF DID NOT OFFER ANY EVIDENCE IN SUPPORT OF HIS CLAIM FOR INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS; DEFENDANT PROVIDED EVIDENCE SHOWING THE REASONABLENESS OF ITS CONDUCT. =(302/JKS/AY)

rulings.Case Title: DANA BUCCHERI VS. LEGALMATCH.COM, A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, OCTOBER 22, 2007. LINE 4. DEFENDANTS LEGALMATCH.COM and MATT GRIFFITH'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion for Summary Adjudication is granted as to the 1st cause of action, sex discrimination. Favoring a paramour is not sex discrimination, 807 F. 2d 304; 41 CA 4th 1626, and plaintiff did not present evidence that the discharge was because of her sex. Motion for Summary Adjudication is denied as to the 2nd cause of action, sexual harrassment. There is a triable issue of fact as to whether there was a hostile work environment. Motion for Summary Adjudication is denied as to the 3rd and 4th causes of action, retaliation and wrongful discharge. The evidence discloses that 1) the discharge occurred shortly after plaintiff complained about the relationship; 2) Griffith was hostile against plaintiff after finding out about the complaints; 3) Griffith had a role in plaintiff's termination. =(302/JKS/PB)

rulings.Case Title: FLORENTINE, LLC VS. 300 BEALE STREET, LLC, et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, OCTOBER 22, 2007. LINE 5. PLAINTIFF FLORENTINE, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is denied. Defendant has raised a triable issue of fact as to whether there was a meeting of the minds on November 15, 2006 at the mediation and whether there is an enforceable agreement. =(302/JKS/AY)
